title: Home
text:
Headshot




Statistics & Data Science Student at UCLA
I’m a UCLA student interested in data analysis, machine learning, and using data to solve real-world problems. My work focuses on building clear insights from complex datasets using Python, R, SQL, Tableau, and statistical modeling.
I’m especially interested in projects that combine technical analysis with real-world impact, including media analytics, social trends, business intelligence, and data visualization.
View My Projects Resume & Contact

title: Home
text:
What I Hope to Build
As I continue developing my experience in statistics and data science, I hope to work on projects that are not only technically strong but also meaningful and accessible. I’m interested in using data to support better decisions, uncover important patterns, and communicate insights clearly to different audiences.
See All Projects →

title: About
text:
I’m a Statistics & Data Science student at UCLA with a strong interest in using data to uncover meaningful patterns and communicate insights clearly. My interest in statistics began in high school when I discovered the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics website. I was fascinated by how freely accessible data could help people make real decisions about education, careers, and the future. It was surprising to me that a public website could contain so much information and quietly influence the paths people choose in life.
As a first-generation college student from a Guatemalan immigrant family, that experience resonated with me deeply. Access to clear, reliable information can make a huge difference when navigating important decisions about education and career paths. Seeing how data could inform those choices showed me how powerful well-organized information can be.
Since then, I’ve been interested in projects that combine technical analysis with real-world relevance—using statistics, machine learning, and data visualization to better understand patterns in the systems that shape everyday life.


University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA)
B.S. in Statistics & Data Science
Expected Graduation: Month Year
Relevant Coursework - Regression Analysis - Machine Learning / Data Analysis - Statistical Computing - Data Visualization - Linear Algebra - Probability / Mathematical Statistics



Many of the problems that interest me most are connected to digital media and the internet. I enjoy exploring questions related to social media platforms, online content ecosystems, and the entertainment industry. Platforms like YouTube, Netflix, and Spotify are built on data-driven systems, and I find it fascinating to study the patterns behind how content spreads, trends emerge, and audiences engage with media.



Outside of coursework, I enjoy working on collaborative data science projects and helping others learn technical tools. As a workshop lead for the National Student Data Corps (NSDC), I have presented SQL and Tableau workshops to groups of students, focusing on making data tools more accessible for beginners.
I’m also a PC gamer and enjoy building computers where I’v actually built my own gaming PC using scholarship funds and savings. I’m a big fan of animated films and appreciate the years of technical and creative work that go into producing them.
I also enjoy working on unconventional data projects. One thing I love about data science is that it can be applied almost anywhere even to unexpected data sets (saw one applied to UFO’s, which was fascinating!)

title: Projects
text:
Overview:
Developed a machine learning model in Python to predict whether a YouTube video would become viral using large-scale metadata and textual features.
My Contribution:
Scraped and processed over 774,000 video records, engineered structured and text-based features, and helped train a neural network model to analyze patterns in video performance.
Tools:
Python, Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, machine learning

title: Projects
text:
Los Angeles Housing Price Analysis
Overview:
Conducted an exploratory analysis of housing prices in Los Angeles using historical housing datasets and geographic data. The project focused on identifying spatial and structural patterns that influence property values.
My Contribution:
Cleaned and processed housing datasets, integrated geographic boundary data for Los Angeles County, and built a regression-based model to examine how location and housing characteristics relate to price trends.
Tools:
List tools, skills, or methods.
Link:
Research Poster

title: Projects
text:
Women Film Directors Analysis (PrimeTime Project)
Overview:
Analyzed gender representation in the film industry by examining datasets of film directors and movie production data. The project focused on identifying disparities in the number of films directed by women across time and production contexts.
My Contribution:
Designed and refined data visualizations to highlight patterns in gender representation among film directors. Transformed raw movie datasets into interpretable charts that supported exploratory analysis and discussion within the research team.
Results:
The project produced an interactive website presenting the analysis and visualizations, helping communicate trends in gender disparity within the film industry.
Tools:
Data visualization, Tableau, exploratory data analysis, collaborative research
Link:
PrimeTime

title: Projects
